mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment signals.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST growth in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in > 60% of cases with 3-month rolling correlation < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 261.775 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 261.775.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) during elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 261.775) creates a stagflationary environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to policy uncertainty.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show positive correlation with combined RSAFS and CPIAUCSL in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through wage-driven consumer demand.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns show negative correlation with combined PAYEMS and UNRATE in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1547 thousand units signals improved construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1547.
